message:
feat(ftui): legacy theme bridge + style tokens (2noh9.3.15)

Add src/ui/theme.rs bridging ratatui ThemePalette to ftui's Theme/StyleSheet:
- CassTheme wraps ftui Theme + StyleSheet + ColorProfile + ThemeFlags
- 30+ named style tokens (text, accent, border, role, status, stripe, gradient)
- ratatui→ftui color conversion for migration (Color + PackedRgba)
- Color interpolation utilities (lerp_color, dim_color)
- Env var detection (NO_COLOR, CASS_NO_*, CASS_A11Y)
- Agent pane styling, role bg styles, zebra stripes
- 24 unit tests passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.16","title":"Task: FocusGraph navigation system on ftui (directional traversal, focus trapping, focus ring)","description":"## Why
The current TUI uses a simple FocusRegion enum (Results/Detail) with manual tracking and focus_flash_until timer. ftui provides a proper FocusGraph for navigation that handles the complexity of modals, nested widgets, and directional movement.

## What
Replace FocusRegion with ftui_widgets::focus::FocusGraph. Define focus nodes:
1. **SearchBar** (text input focus)
2. **FilterPills** (each pill focusable for edit/delete)
3. **ResultsList** (item selection focus)
4. **DetailPane** (scroll focus)
5. **CommandPalette** (modal focus trap)
6. **ExportModal** (modal focus trap)
7. **HelpOverlay** (modal focus trap)

FocusGraph handles:
- Directional navigation (Up from Results → SearchBar, Down from SearchBar → Results)
- Tab/Shift+Tab cycling through all nodes
- FocusTrap ensures modal focus doesn't leak to background widgets
- Visible focus ring indicator (colored border or glow effect) on focused widget

## Implementation
- Use frame.register_hit() for each focusable region
- FocusTrap activated when any modal is open
- Focus ring rendered as themed border highlight (uses agent accent colors)
- Replaces manual focus_region tracking and focus_flash_until timer

## Tests
- Unit: FocusGraph navigation sequence tests (Tab cycles, directional moves)
- Unit: Modal focus trapping prevents escape
- Snapshot: Focus ring renders correctly in each theme
- E2E: Full keyboard navigation flow (search → results → detail → modal → back)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All existing focus transitions preserved (no regression)
- Tab order matches logical UI flow
- Modals trap focus; Esc releases
- Focus ring visible in all 6+ themes with WCAG contrast
- CASS_DISABLE_ANIMATIONS=1 disables focus transition animations

## Depends On
- 2noh9.3.1 (layout foundation must exist first)
- 2noh9.2.3 (Program skeleton must be running)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:18.698317676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:29.107507028Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:29.107442839Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:18.698317676Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.16","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:26.431410925Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.17","title":"Task: Clipboard integration via OSC52 on ftui (copy paths, content, code blocks; works over SSH)","description":"## Why
The current TUI has basic clipboard via crossterm Paste events, but lacks reliable cross-terminal clipboard write support. OSC52 is the escape sequence that allows terminal apps to read/write the system clipboard without external dependencies — critical for multi-machine search (works over SSH).

## What
Leverage ftui-extras/clipboard for OSC52 clipboard integration:
1. **Copy selected result path+line** to clipboard (y or Ctrl+Y)
2. **Copy detail pane content** to clipboard (Ctrl+C in detail view without selection)
3. **Copy code block** from detail view (keybinding on specific code block)
4. **Copy search query** to clipboard
5. **Paste from clipboard** into search bar (Ctrl+V — unified API replacing raw crossterm Paste)

## Implementation
- ftui-extras clipboard module handles terminal capability detection (OSC52 support probe)
- Graceful fallback: if OSC52 not supported, try xclip/xsel/pbcopy
- Visual feedback: brief toast notification on successful copy ("Copied path to clipboard")
- Multi-select copy: Ctrl+Y copies all selected item paths separated by newlines

## Tests
- Unit: OSC52 escape sequence generation is correct
- Unit: Fallback chain works (OSC52 → xclip → noop with warning)
- Integration: Copy→paste roundtrip with ProgramSimulator
- E2E: Copy result path, verify clipboard content

## Acceptance Criteria
- Works over SSH sessions (OSC52 passthrough)
- Falls back gracefully on terminals without OSC52
- Toast confirms copy operation
- All existing y/Ctrl+Y behavior preserved

## Depends On
- 2noh9.2.3 (Program skeleton must be running)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:34.903656751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:33.479806754Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.17","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:00:33.479689506Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.17","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:34.903656751Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","title":"Task: Search bar UX on ftui (text input, history, suggestions, debounce, match modes)","description":"## Goal
Recreate and improve the search bar experience:
- search-as-you-type with debounce (still sub-60ms perceived latency)
- Up/Down history navigation + de-dup
- Ctrl key edits (clear, delete token)
- Enter forces immediate search
- Match modes (standard/prefix) and wildcard fallback indicator
- Search modes (lexical/semantic/hybrid) toggle

## Implementation Notes
- Use `ftui-widgets::input` or `textarea` for editable text.
- State transitions should be reducer-style and unit-testable.
- Search execution should be cancelable/coalesced (avoid piling requests during typing).

## Tests
- Unit tests for query editing + history behavior.
- Deterministic simulation tests using `ftui_runtime::ProgramSimulator` for:
  - typing "auth" and seeing results update after debounce
  - Up/Down history nav

## Acceptance Criteria
- Behavior matches parity matrix for all documented keybindings.
- No input lag or dropped keystrokes under heavy rendering.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:28:10.964810150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:41.442234617Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ftui","search","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:41.039710098Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:41.442165218Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:43:40.617283649Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2noh9.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:28:10.964810150Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.10","title":"Task: Cost estimation (USD) from model_pricing + coverage diagnostics","description":"## Goal
Compute *historical* estimated USD costs from token usage using the existing SQLite analytics schema:
- per-message ledger: `token_usage.estimated_cost_usd`
- daily rollups: `token_daily_stats.estimated_cost_usd`
- conversation summaries: `conversations.estimated_cost_usd` (and friends)
- pricing table: `model_pricing` (pattern match + effective_date)

This must be:
- correct across time (pricing changes)
- deterministic (stable pattern selection)
- coverage-aware (never “fake precision” when pricing/model is unknown)

## Context (Current Reality)
- Ingest currently inserts `token_usage` rows with token components + model_name/provider/etc.
- `model_pricing` is already seeded, but cost is not computed (cost column left NULL/0).
- `token_daily_stats` includes `estimated_cost_usd` but is not populated.

## Design Requirements
1. **Pricing selection** (deterministic)
   - Match: `token_usage.model_name LIKE model_pricing.model_pattern` (and provider match when available).
   - Effective date: pick the row with the greatest `effective_date` that is <= message timestamp date.
   - Specificity tie-break: prefer the most specific pattern (longest pattern, or an explicit `priority` column if we add one).

2. **Component costs**
   - input: `input_tokens * input_cost_per_mtok / 1_000_000`
   - output: `output_tokens * output_cost_per_mtok / 1_000_000`
   - cache_read: if price present, include; else flag missing.
   - cache_creation: if price present, include; else flag missing.
   - thinking tokens: only compute if/when providers price it (usually not priced separately).

3. **Coverage / “no fake precision”**
   - If no pricing row is found:
     - `estimated_cost_usd` must be NULL (not 0.0), and we must track pricing coverage counts in queries.
   - If only some components are priced:
     - Either (A) compute partial USD and flag partial coverage, OR (B) treat as NULL.
     - Choose one policy and document it in robot-docs; whichever we choose must be stable.

4. **Performance**
   - Ingest path: cost computation must be O(inserted_messages) and avoid per-row SQL lookups where possible.
   - Backfill path: must run chunked and be able to handle millions of rows.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Pricing lookup utility**
   - Add `pricing::lookup_model_price(model_name, provider, timestamp_ms)` that returns the chosen pricing row + diagnostics.
   - Add tests for ambiguous patterns and effective_date selection.

2. **Compute per-message USD**
   - During ingest (when building `TokenUsageEntry`), compute `estimated_cost_usd` using the lookup.
   - For backfill/rebuild (z9fse.13), recompute deterministically from messages -> token_usage.

3. **Roll up USD**
   - Update `TokenStatsAggregator` to sum `estimated_cost_usd` into `token_daily_stats.estimated_cost_usd`.
   - Update conversation summary updater (`update_conversation_token_summaries_in_tx`) to set `conversations.estimated_cost_usd` as SUM of non-null `token_usage.estimated_cost_usd`.

4. **Diagnostics**
   - Provide counts for:
     - priced vs unpriced messages
     - top unknown model names
     - partial pricing cases (if we allow partial USD)

## Acceptance Criteria
- token_usage rows created by ingest have correct `estimated_cost_usd` when pricing exists.
- token_daily_stats estimated_cost_usd equals SUM(token_usage.estimated_cost_usd) for that day/model_family slice.
- conversation estimated_cost_usd equals SUM(token_usage.estimated_cost_usd) for that conversation.
- Coverage diagnostics clearly report unknown models/pricing gaps.

## Testing & Logging
- Unit tests:
  - pricing selection (pattern specificity + effective_date)
  - arithmetic correctness and rounding
- Integration tests:
  - seed model_pricing + insert token_usage rows -> verify ledger + rollup + conversation summaries
- E2E scripts (extend z9fse.8):
  - index fixture -> `cass analytics cost --json` (once implemented) validates USD totals + coverage output
- Logging:
  - debug-level logs for pricing misses (sampled/throttled, not spammy)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:50:49.571304606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:31:29.184577465Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","cli","cost","pricing","schema","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:46Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:46Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.10","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:46Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.11","title":"Task: Add model dimension to Track A analytics (message_metrics + model rollups)","description":"## Goal
Add **model-aware analytics** to Track A so we can slice tokens by model across:
- time (hour/day/week/month)
- agent types
- workspaces/projects
- sources (local vs remotes)

This enables `cass analytics models --json` and model-driven dashboards without relying on Track B joins.

## Storage (Schema v12)
1. Extend `message_metrics` with model fields extracted at ingest:
- `model_name TEXT` (raw)
- `model_family TEXT` (normalized; e.g. claude/gpt/gemini/unknown)
- `model_tier TEXT` (normalized; e.g. sonnet/opus/flash/o3/unknown)
- `provider TEXT` (anthropic/openai/google/unknown)

2. Add model rollups (do NOT mutate existing usage_* PKs):
- `usage_models_daily(day_id, agent_slug, workspace_id, source_id, model_family, model_tier)`
  - columns: message_count, assistant_message_count, api_tokens_total (+components), content_tokens_est_total, api_coverage_message_count, last_updated
- (optional) `usage_models_hourly` if we find day->hour rollups are needed for interactive model burn-rate.

Indexes must support:
- time-range scans by (model_family, day_id)
- filtering by agent_slug/workspace_id/source_id

## Ingest
- Populate model fields from `connectors::extract_tokens_for_agent()`:
  - store raw `model_name`
  - normalize into (family, tier, provider) using `connectors::normalize_model()`
- Record into model rollup aggregator and upsert in-batch (same pattern as usage_*).

## Rebuild / Backfill
- Extend analytics rebuild so it also populates:
  - model columns in `message_metrics`
  - model rollups from the rebuilt fact table

## Acceptance Criteria
- Ingest writes non-null model_family/tier/provider where model_name is known.
- Rebuild produces identical model rollups to live ingest on the same fixture DB.
- Coverage diagnostics can report:
  - % messages with model_name
  - top unknown/"unknown" models bucket

## Testing & Logging
- Migration test: v12 adds expected columns/tables/indexes.
- Integration: fixture with 2 models across 2 days verifies rollup sums and determinism.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.12","title":"Task: Analytics query library (shared by CLI + ftui dashboards)","description":"## Goal
Create a reusable analytics query layer (a small internal library) so we do NOT duplicate analytics logic across:
- `cass analytics ...` CLI (robot JSON)
- ftui analytics dashboards (interactive TUI)
- pages export analytics generator (optional future unification)

This is the “single source of truth” for bucket semantics, filters, week/month logic, derived metrics, and stable ordering.

## Why This Matters
If we implement analytics queries directly inside CLI handlers and then again inside the TUI, we will drift. Drift here is catastrophic because users will see different token totals depending on which view they use.

The query layer should be:
- fast: prefer rollups (`usage_hourly`/`usage_daily`, plus tool/model rollups when they exist)
- deterministic: stable ordering + stable rounding rules
- explicit about semantics: UTC boundaries, ISO week rules, coverage definition

## Proposed API (Rust)
Create `crate::analytics` with submodules:

1. `crate::analytics::types`
   - Filter structs: `AnalyticsFilter { since, until, agents[], workspaces[], sources[], roles[] }`
   - Grouping enum: `GroupBy { Hour, Day, Week, Month }`
   - Dimension enum: `Dim { Agent, Workspace, Source, Role, Model, Tool }`
   - Metric enum: `Metric { ApiTotal, ApiInput, ApiOutput, ContentEstTotal, ToolCalls, PlanCount, CoveragePct, UsdEstTotal, ... }`
   - Output structs for robot JSON (serde Serialize)

2. `crate::analytics::query`
   - `query_tokens_timeseries(conn, filter, group_by, metric_set) -> Timeseries`
   - `query_breakdown(conn, filter, dim, metric, limit) -> Vec<Row>`
   - `query_tools(conn, filter, group_by, metric_set, limit) -> ToolReport` (served from tool rollups when available)
   - `query_status(conn) -> AnalyticsStatus`

3. `crate::analytics::bucketing`
   - `hour_id_from_millis`, `day_id_from_millis` (already exist in storage)
   - helpers to map day_id -> iso_week_id and day_id -> month_id (UTC)

4. `crate::analytics::derive`
   - derived averages computed safely (divide-by-zero safe, explicit nulls)
   - token component composition (`api_total = in+out+cache_read+cache_creation+thinking`)

## Semantics (Must Be Written Down)
- All buckets are UTC.
- Weeks are ISO-8601 weeks (Mon start). Output key must include both `iso_year` and `iso_week`.
- Months are calendar months UTC (year + month).
- Coverage definition: `api_coverage_message_count / message_count` where “api coverage message” means `api_data_source == 'api'`.

## Implementation Constraints
- Prefer rollup tables:
  - hour queries read `usage_hourly`
  - day/week/month queries read `usage_daily` and aggregate (week/month)
  - tools queries read tool rollups (z9fse.6) when present
- If a request asks for a metric not representable from rollups, either:
  - explicitly reject with a clear error, OR
  - require `--deep` and use message_metrics scan with visible perf warning + `_meta.path="slow"`

## Acceptance Criteria
- CLI analytics commands (z9fse.3.*) call this module (no duplicated SQL in CLI handlers).
- ftui analytics views use the same module for DB-backed queries.
- Unit tests cover:
  - week/month boundary logic
  - derived metric math
  - ordering stability (bucket sorting)
- Integration tests seed rollup rows and verify query outputs exactly.

## Testing & Logging
- Unit: boundary tests for iso weeks (year transitions) and months.
- Integration: create a small in-memory SQLite DB with rollup rows and assert outputs.
- Logging: debug spans include query name, filters, rows scanned, elapsed_ms.","notes":"DEFERRED: Build CLI commands directly first (z9fse.3.x). Extract shared query library only when the TUI dashboard (2noh9.4.18) actually needs it. Premature abstraction risk.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:12:09.691319060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:31:42.828231635Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","architecture"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.12","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:12:09.691319060Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.13","title":"Task: Make analytics tracks coherent (rebuild covers token_usage/token_daily_stats + summaries)","description":"## Goal
Eliminate drift between analytics **Track A** and **Track B** so any rebuild/backfill produces a coherent, deterministic analytics state.

## Current Reality (2026-02-06)
- **Track A (v11)**: `message_metrics` + `usage_hourly` + `usage_daily`.
  - `SqliteStorage::rebuild_analytics()` currently clears + rebuilds **only Track A**.
- **Track B (v10)**: `token_usage` + `token_daily_stats` + conversation token summary columns (`conversations.total_*`, `grand_total_tokens`, `estimated_cost_usd`, `primary_model`, etc.).
  - Live ingest currently populates Track B.
  - Rebuild/backfill does **not** rebuild Track B.

Result: after running rebuild/backfill, Track B can be stale/inconsistent with Track A.

## Decision (v1)
- Keep both tracks for now (Track A drives general time-series; Track B carries model/pricing/cost + per-conversation summaries).
- Make rebuild/backfill **explicit** about which tracks are rebuilt and provide an **all-tracks** option.
- Add drift detection + invariants so drift cannot regress silently.

## Deliverables
1. **Rebuild API with track selection**
   - Implement rebuild modes:
     - Track A only (existing)
     - Track B only
     - All tracks (A then B)
   - Implementation options:
     - extend `SqliteStorage::rebuild_analytics()` with a mode enum, OR
     - add `rebuild_analytics_track_a()`, `rebuild_analytics_track_b()`, `rebuild_analytics_all()`.

2. **Track B rebuild/backfill**
   - Clear/rebuild in a transaction (chunked for large DBs):
     - clear `token_usage`
     - clear `token_daily_stats`
     - reset conversation summary columns to NULL/0
   - Recompute `token_usage` deterministically from messages by:
     - joining `messages` + `conversations` + `agents` + `workspaces`
     - calling `connectors::extract_tokens_for_agent(...)`
     - writing one ledger row per message with `data_source` preserved (`api` vs `estimated`)
   - Recompute `token_daily_stats` to match ingest semantics (prefer reusing `TokenStatsAggregator` so codepaths cannot drift).
   - Recompute conversation summary columns from `token_usage` (must match `update_conversation_token_summaries_in_tx`).

3. **Meta + drift signals**
   - Record meta keys (names tbd, but stable):
     - last rebuild timestamp
     - which tracks were rebuilt (`a|b|all`)
     - schema versions
   - Drift signals used by:
     - `cass analytics status --json`
     - `cass analytics validate --json`

4. **Invariants / guardrails**
   - Track A: rollups match `SUM(message_metrics)` for sampled buckets.
   - Track B: `token_daily_stats` matches `SUM(token_usage)` for the same (day, agent_slug, source_id, model_family) slice.
   - Conversation summaries match `SUM(token_usage)` per conversation.
   - Sanity: no negative counters; `api_coverage_message_count <= message_count`, etc.

## Acceptance Criteria
- After `rebuild(all)` on a fixture DB:
  - Track A + Track B row counts are non-zero and stable across two runs (idempotent).
  - Drift detector reports `possible=false`.
  - Validate invariants pass.
- On an intentionally drifted DB (e.g. delete `token_usage` rows):
  - status reports drift and recommends action.
  - validate returns actionable failure output.

## Testing & Logging
- Integration: small DB spanning 2 days + 2 agents + 2 workspaces; rebuild(all) passes invariants.
- Integration: drift injection test (delete/alter one table) is detected.
- Logging: per-track rebuild spans include rows/sec + total rows; avoid per-row spam.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:14:56.486748513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:32:50.317497864Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","correctness","performance","schema"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.13","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:14:56.486748513Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.14","title":"Task: Plan analytics v2 (token attribution + heuristic refinement + rollup support)","description":"## Goal
Upgrade plan analytics from **message counts** (v1) to **token attribution** + better heuristics so cass can answer:
- plan token share (how much of a day/week/month was planning vs doing)
- avg tokens per plan message / per plan event
- trends by agent/workspace/source

This is required for high-quality "tokens per plan" benchmarks and for the ftui Plans view (`2noh9.4.18.12`).

## Current State (v1, already in code)
- `message_metrics.has_plan` exists (cheap heuristic).
- `usage_hourly/usage_daily.plan_message_count` exists.
- There are tests for plan heuristic detection (see z9fse.7).

Gaps:
- We cannot attribute **tokens** to planning; only message counts.
- The heuristic is intentionally simple and will have false positives/negatives across agents.

## Deliverables
### 1) Define "Plan" Semantics (Documented)
V2 must explicitly define what counts as planning:
- default: assistant messages that contain a plan section (role == assistant)
- clarify whether to include tool messages or user planning prompts (default: exclude)
- define whether "plan" is:
  - message-level (good enough for v2.0)
  - section-level (optional v2.1)
  - multi-message plan events (optional v3)

### 2) Token Attribution (v2.0)
For each message, compute plan-attributed tokens as:
- if `has_plan == 1`:
  - `plan_content_tokens_est = content_tokens_est`
  - `plan_api_tokens = api_tokens_total` (when api-sourced)
- else: 0

This yields:
- `plan_token_share_content = plan_content_tokens_est_total / content_tokens_est_total`
- `plan_token_share_api = plan_api_tokens_total / api_tokens_total` (coverage-aware)

### 3) Storage Options (Pick One)
We need O(#buckets) query-time for plan token share.

**Option A (preferred if schema churn is acceptable): add columns to existing rollups**
- Add to `usage_hourly` and `usage_daily`:
  - `plan_content_tokens_est_total`
  - `plan_api_tokens_total`
  - (optional) component breakdowns if needed for cost attribution later

**Option B: add dedicated plan rollup tables**
- `plan_usage_hourly(hour_id, agent_slug, workspace_id, source_id)`
- `plan_usage_daily(day_id, agent_slug, workspace_id, source_id)`
- Keep `usage_*` unchanged; queries join (or return both sections).

Selection criteria:
- Option A makes tokens command simpler but requires careful migration/upsert updates.
- Option B avoids touching stable PK tables but adds join complexity.

### 4) Ingest + Rebuild
- Ingest: when constructing `message_metrics`, compute plan-attributed token deltas and feed into the chosen rollup strategy.
- Rebuild: must recompute plan rollups deterministically from rebuilt `message_metrics`.

### 5) Heuristic Refinement (v2.1)
Improve `has_plan` without going LLM-heavy:
- Add a curated corpus across agents (Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini) of:
  - true plan messages
  - false positives (e.g., tool output listing steps)
  - false negatives (implicit plans)
- Heuristic improvements (examples):
  - require both a plan header/intent marker AND a structured list of steps
  - down-weight if content is dominated by tool output markers
  - add an upper bound on plan header distance from start of message

We must track heuristic versioning (meta key) so rebuild results are reproducible.

## Acceptance Criteria
- On fixtures spanning multiple buckets:
  - plan token share metrics are computed and stable.
  - divide-by-zero safe: share is null when denom=0.
  - coverage-aware: API share is null/explicit when api_tokens_total=0.
- Rebuild is idempotent: two rebuild runs produce identical plan rollups.
- Heuristic refinement reduces false positives on the curated corpus (target thresholds documented in test).

## Testing & Logging
- Unit: heuristic corpus tests + boundary cases.
- Integration: fixture DB verifies rollups == sum(message_metrics) for plan-attributed totals.
- Logging: debug-only sampling of plan-detection misses (throttled), plus rebuild spans with rows/sec.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:16:29.108794301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T09:06:45.389265178Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","schema","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T08:16:29.108794301Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.14","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T09:06:45.389133553Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","title":"Epic: Analytics CLI + Robot Contract (cass analytics …)","description":"Epic for the complete robot-first analytics CLI surface (`cass analytics ...`).

Deliver a stable, machine-operable command family that exposes analytics health, token time series, tool/model/cost breakdowns, rebuild workflows, and validation checks with deterministic JSON contracts. The CLI must share semantics with the analytics query library and FrankenTUI dashboards to prevent drift.

This epic also includes robot-docs contract publishing and test coverage that locks command behavior, schema stability, and failure diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:47:56.690284560Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:30:43.541374393Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["analytics","cli","docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:46Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:46Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:46Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":193,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Analytics CLI Contract (Robot-First)

This comment is the canonical CLI/JSON contract for the analytics surface. The intent is: **agents can use analytics without reading code**.

## Command Tree (v1)
- `cass analytics status --json`
- `cass analytics tokens --json`
- `cass analytics models --json`
- `cass analytics cost --json`
- `cass analytics rebuild --json`
- `cass analytics validate --json`

Notes:
- Subcommand names and flag names must remain stable once published.
- stdout = JSON data only; stderr = diagnostics.

## Common Flags (all analytics subcommands)
- Time window:
  - `--since <time>` / `--until <time>` (ISO, relative like -7d, keywords today/yesterday)
  - convenience presets: `--days N`, `--today`, `--week`
- Filters:
  - `--agent <slug>` (repeatable or comma list)
  - `--workspace <path>` (repeatable or comma list)
  - `--source <local|remote|all|source_id>`
  - `--role <user|assistant|tool|system|all>` (advanced; may require deep/slow path)

## Output: `analytics status`
Purpose: a fast, high-signal snapshot for both humans and automation.

Suggested JSON shape:
```json
{
  "db_path": "...",
  "tables": {
    "message_metrics": {"rows": 123, "min_ts": 0, "max_ts": 0},
    "usage_hourly": {"rows": 0, "last_updated": 0},
    "usage_daily": {"rows": 0, "last_updated": 0},
    "token_usage": {"rows": 0, "min_ts": 0, "max_ts": 0},
    "token_daily_stats": {"rows": 0, "last_updated": 0}
  },
  "coverage": {
    "api_usage_message_pct": 0.0,
    "model_name_message_pct": 0.0,
    "notes": ["..."]
  },
  "drift": {
    "possible": false,
    "signals": ["..."],
    "recommended_action": "none|rebuild_all|rebuild_message_metrics|rebuild_token_usage"
  },
  "_meta": {"elapsed_ms": 0}
}
```

## Output: `analytics tokens`
Purpose: time-series usage.

Required capabilities:
- group-by: hour/day/week/month (UTC)
- dims: agent/workspace/source
- metrics:
  - api tokens: total/input/output/cache_read/cache_creation/thinking
  - content tokens: content_tokens_est_total
  - counts: message_count, user_message_count, assistant_message_count, tool_call_count, plan_message_count
  - coverage: api_coverage_pct
- derived:
  - api tokens per assistant msg
  - content tokens per user msg
  - tool calls per 1k tokens
  - planning share (once v2 plan attribution exists)

Response should be stable and ordered:
```json
{
  "group_by": "day",
  "filters": {"since": "...", "until": "...", "agent": ["claude_code"], "workspace": [], "source": "all"},
  "buckets": [
    {"key": "2026-02-01", "metrics": {"api_tokens_total": 0, "content_tokens_est_total": 0, "message_count": 0, "api_coverage_pct": 0.0}, "derived": {"api_tokens_per_assistant_msg": null}}
  ],
  "_meta": {"elapsed_ms": 0, "path": "rollup|slow_path", "warning": null}
}
```

If we must scan `message_metrics` (slow path), `_meta.path` must say so.

## Output: `analytics models`
- top models by API tokens and/or content-est tokens
- model coverage (percentage of messages with model_name)
- unknown bucket must be explicit

## Output: `analytics cost`
- estimated USD totals using `model_pricing`
- coverage diagnostics:
  - unknown pricing models
  - estimate-only vs api-usage
- must never present USD with false precision

## Output: `analytics rebuild`
- must be explicit about what is rebuilt:
  - Track A: message_metrics + usage_* rollups
  - Track B: token_usage + token_daily_stats + conversation summary columns

If we support `--all` / `--track a|b`, document it here.

## Output: `analytics validate`
- invariants results with actionable errors
- include sample bucket IDs and expected vs actual for failures

## Implementation Constraint
All query semantics (bucket boundaries, week/month rules, derived metrics) must live in `crate::analytics` (z9fse.12) so CLI and FrankenTUI dashboards cannot drift.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.1","title":"Task: CLI scaffolding for analytics command tree (clap + help + docs hook)","description":"## Objective
Build the production-grade analytics CLI skeleton so all analytics commands share one stable robot contract and one centralized argument model.

This task is the hard foundation for every downstream analytics command (`status`, `tokens`, `tools`, `models`, `cost`, `rebuild`, `validate`). If this shape is wrong, every later task drifts.

## Why This Matters
- Gives other agents a deterministic command surface they can depend on.
- Prevents duplicated flag parsing and inconsistent JSON behavior.
- Makes analytics discoverable via `--help` and `robot-docs` before deep features land.

## Scope
1. Add `cass analytics` command tree in clap wiring.
2. Register subcommands with stable names:
   - `status`
   - `tokens`
   - `tools`
   - `models`
   - `cost`
   - `rebuild`
   - `validate`
3. Define shared analytics flags and parsing model:
   - time range (`--since`, `--until`, `--days`)
   - dimensional filters (`--agent`, `--workspace`, `--source`)
   - bucketing (`--group-by hour|day|week|month`) where relevant
   - output mode (`--json` and robot-safe behavior)
4. Add dispatch stubs for each subcommand (even if implementation is deferred).
5. Wire placeholder hooks for `cass robot-docs analytics` topics so docs work can attach to a stable route.

## Contract Rules
- `stdout` must be data-only JSON in robot mode.
- Human diagnostics must go to `stderr`.
- Exit-code semantics must follow existing cass conventions.
- Do not introduce ad-hoc per-command parsing; all analytics commands must reuse shared parser structs/helpers.

## Test + Logging Requirements
- Unit tests for clap parsing:
  - command aliases and required args
  - repeated filter args
  - invalid enum values produce actionable errors
- E2E CLI tests (non-interactive) for:
  - `cass analytics --help`
  - each subcommand help
  - `--json` mode parseability and deterministic top-level schema envelope
- Failure logs must include command, exit code, stdout, stderr, and elapsed milliseconds.

## Out of Scope
- Computing analytics data itself (owned by child command tasks and query library work).","acceptance_criteria":"1. `cass analytics --help` lists all required subcommands and shared analytics flags with consistent wording.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.2","title":"Task: CLI analytics status (row counts, freshness, coverage, drift warnings)","description":"## Objective
Implement `cass analytics status --json` as the canonical health/quality endpoint for analytics data.

   - Track A: `message_metrics`, `usage_hourly`, `usage_daily`
   - Track B: `token_usage`, `token_daily_stats`, plus conversation summary coverage signals
2. For each table include high-signal metadata:
   - row counts
   - min/max timestamp coverage where applicable
   - last update timestamp
3. Report coverage diagnostics (not just totals):
   - API-token coverage %
   - model-name coverage %
   - estimate-only share
   - unknown/empty-model counts
4. Detect drift and coherence failures between tracks:
   - one track fresh while other is stale
   - missing rollups while source facts exist
   - rebuild mode mismatch symptoms
5. Emit recommended remediation action in output:
   - `none`
   - `rebuild_all`
   - `rebuild_track_a`
   - `rebuild_track_b`

## Output Contract
- Deterministic, machine-parseable JSON schema with explicit sections:
  - `tables`
  - `coverage`
  - `drift`
  - `_meta`
- No hidden heuristics; include concrete drift signals list so operators understand *why* a warning fired.

## UX Requirements
- Fast path (<~200ms on medium DBs, barring cold I/O).
- Human-readable warnings on stderr in non-json mode; JSON stays clean in stdout.

  - coverage math edge cases (zero rows, partial rows)

  - healthy

  - stale Track B
  - contradictory row-count/timestamp states
- E2E tests for command output stability:
  - parse JSON and verify required keys exist
  - verify recommended action under seeded drift states

  - fixture profile
  - computed drift signals
  - emitted recommendation","acceptance_criteria":"1. `cass analytics status --json` returns a stable schema containing table stats, coverage metrics, drift signals, and recommended action.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.5","title":"Task: CLI analytics validate (rollup invariants + drift detection)","description":"## Objective
Implement `cass analytics validate --json` as the correctness gate for analytics math and rollup integrity.

`status` tells us if data *looks* healthy; `validate` must prove key invariants actually hold.

## Scope
1. Implement fast invariant checks (default mode):
   - non-negative counters/tokens
   - required key columns present and non-null for aggregate keys
   - rollup rows exist for periods with source facts
2. Implement aggregate consistency checks:
   - sampled bucket sums in rollups equal recomputed sums from fact tables within tolerance
   - per-dimension totals reconcile with global totals
3. Implement cross-track drift checks:
   - Track A totals vs Track B totals are directionally consistent where overlap exists
   - identify stale/partial recomputation patterns
4. Provide two validation depths:
   - `--mode fast` (default): high-signal checks under ~1s on fixture DB
   - `--mode deep`: broader recomputation windows for CI/nightly confidence

## Output Contract
Return deterministic JSON with:
- `summary` (pass/fail + severity)
- `checks` array (id, status, measured, expected, tolerance, message)
- `drift_signals`
- `recommendations` (specific actions, not generic)
- `_meta` (elapsed_ms, mode, sample strategy)

## Performance + Safety
- Must be read-only and safe on live DB.
- Must fail closed: if a check cannot execute, report explicit `error` status rather than silently passing.

## Test + Logging Requirements
- Unit tests per check function and tolerance logic.
- Integration tests with intentionally corrupted fixture datasets:
  - off-by-one rollup
  - negative token rows
  - stale track mismatch
- E2E tests:
  - validate success case returns `pass`
  - seeded failure case returns `fail` with actionable checks
- Logging on failure must include:
  - failed check id
  - measured vs expected values
  - bucket/dimension keys used in comparison","acceptance_criteria":"1. `cass analytics validate --json` emits stable JSON listing individual checks and an overall pass/fail summary.

4. Cross-track drift and rollup mismatch errors are clearly diagnosable from output alone.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-z9fse.3.7","title":"Task: CLI analytics cost (USD estimates + pricing coverage)","description":"## Objective
Implement `cass analytics cost --json` for transparent USD estimation with explicit uncertainty and pricing coverage diagnostics.

Cost numbers are high-stakes; the command must never imply precision that the data does not support.

## Scope
1. Compute estimated cost from model pricing and token usage:
   - input/output/cache/thinking token components when available
   - explicit fallback rules when only partial token classes are present
2. Provide breakdowns by:
   - time bucket (day/week/month)
   - model family / model
   - agent/workspace/source filters
3. Emit pricing coverage quality:
   - % token volume matched to known pricing
   - list of unknown/unpriced models and affected token share
   - estimate-only warnings when API usage is sparse
4. Provide derived economics metrics:
   - cost per 1k API tokens
   - cost per assistant message
   - period-over-period delta

## Output Contract
Deterministic JSON sections:
- `filters`
- `pricing_coverage`
- `buckets`
- `breakdowns`
- `totals`
- `_meta`

Every monetary value should include explicit units (`usd`) and nullable semantics where computation is impossible.

## Precision / Safety Rules
- Use decimal-safe handling appropriate for currency.
- Avoid hidden rounding; round only for presentation layers while retaining raw numeric fields.
- Expose caveats in output when cost is partial.

## Test + Logging Requirements
- Unit tests:
  - pricing rule matching
  - cost math across token classes
  - rounding and nullability behavior
- Integration tests:
  - mixed priced/unpriced model datasets
  - sparse API token coverage
  - period breakdown correctness
